danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to any fleet or colonies in the system; system becomes hazardous

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy fleet movement vectors and colony establishment in contested space
danger_signals.meaning: Political/military threat; rival faction securing resources and territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Advanced ship designs and weapon systems in combat visualization
danger_signals.meaning: Military disadvantage; empire is outmatched in long-term conflict potential

danger_signals.visual_form: Mineral/organic/radioactive content indicators decreasing on planet displays
danger_signals.meaning: Economic vulnerability; colony cannot sustain growth or production

danger_signals.visual_form: Aggressive unit behavior, rapid expansion patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Early-game military threat from aggressive faction

safety_signals.visual_form: Positive condition indicators on colonized worlds
safety_signals.meaning: Population happiness and reproduction rates are favorable; stable colony

safety_signals.visual_form: High mineral/organic/radioactive content values on planets
safety_signals.meaning: Economic security; colony can sustain production and growth

safety_signals.visual_form: Diplomatic status indicators showing 'peaceful' with rival races
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced immediate military threat; negotiation pathway available

safety_signals.visual_form: Limited warp point connections between systems
safety_signals.meaning: Geographic protection; reduced external threat exposure

safety_signals.visual_form: Ship components and facilities with defensive ratings in tech tree
safety_signals.meaning: Military capability to defend colonies and fleets

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Standard human colonial architecture and ship designs

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Expansionist settlement patterns; rapid colony establishment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wide territorial spread; multiple colonized systems

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Advanced technological ship components and facilities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: High-tech research indicators; superior late-game unit designs

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Aggressive military unit behavior; swarm attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Early-game military dominance; rapid fleet expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Xi-Chung fleet presence and rapid expansion territorial claims
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aggressive military expeditions; colony attacks; threat escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple faction colonies across galaxy; contested warp point systems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Diplomatic maneuvering; resource competition; fleet positioning for conflict

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Advanced weapon systems and ship designs; Eee Consortium technological superiority evident
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Strategic warfare; planetary-scale destruction capabilities; system manipulation (black holes, storms)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Peace indicators across all faction relationships; unified alliance status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cessation of hostile actions; coordinated diplomatic engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destroyed rival home worlds and colonies; empty systems; military fleet dominance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Active destruction of enemy infrastructure; fleet combat operations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Star system marked with hazard indicator; visual distortion effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fleet avoidance behavior; colonies evacuating if present

environmental_storytelling.example: A planet with high mineral content but poor conditions tells story of marginal colony struggling despite resource wealth
Praetorian Empire's wide spread of colonies communicates their expansionist nature and peaceful approach to conquest
Systems connected by multiple warp points become contested zones; isolated systems show safe backwater territories

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ship design evolution through tech tree
environmental_storytelling.example: Visual progression from basic ships to devastation-class weapons communicates technological advancement and power scaling
Organic Armor, Religious Talisman, Allegiance Subverter components visually distinguish empires pursuing specialized tech lines
Presence of ice/rock/gas planets with varied atmospheres creates sense of diverse galactic ecology

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Steady colony establishment across multiple systems; non-aggressive first contact
Aggressive military expansion; early conflict initiation; dangerous early-game threat
Diminished threat as other empires catch up technologically; becomes less competitive
Technological superiority manifests in advanced fleet designs; becomes formidable late-game opponent
Forced choice between facility construction (economic growth) or military ship building (defense)
Automatic inter-rival warfare; creates dynamic multi-faction battlefield independent of player actions

notes: Space Empires IV communicates world-state primarily through abstract numerical indicators (resource values, condition ratings, tech tree progression) rather than visual spectacle, consistent with old-school 4X strategy paradigm. Danger/safety signaling is implicit in game mechanics rather than explicit visual design. Faction identity is conveyed through behavioral patterns (expansion style, technological focus, aggression timing) and available unique technologies rather than distinct visual aesthetics. The game prioritizes information density and strategic clarity over immersive visual communication. Political situations emerge from faction relationships and territorial positioning rather than explicit visual indicators.
